content_en:
In the chapter wherein we relate the description of Paradise, we will mention this hadith with its different chains and with other narrations that attest to it. And we seek help from Allah #é.

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 257

The Bodies Of The Prophets Do Not Decompose In The Earth

Umaamah ibn Al-Mundhir 4 reported that the Messenger of Allah #& said, “The chief of the days is Friday. It is the most superior with Allah, more superior with Allah than the day of Al-Fitr and the day of Al-Adhaa. It has in it five qualities: during it, Allah created Adam; during it Allah made Adam die; during it is an hour, in which no slave asks anything from Allah, except that Allah gives it to him - as long as he does not ask for something Haram; and on it, the Hour will arrive. Every Mugarrib (chosen and close) angel, every heaven, every earth, every mountain, and every sea Yushfiqu (fears) Friday (Le, fears that on the Friday before them, the Hour will arrive).” (Ahmad) Ibn Maajah related it as well, The following is a narration that Ibn ‘Umar 4% ascribed to the Prophet i: “The Hour will arrive at the Fajr Aadhaan, on Friday.” At-Tabaraanee related this narration. In AtTadhkirah, Abu ‘Abdullah Al-Qurtubee related that it is on a Friday, halfway through Ramadan. But this requires some proof.

Al-Hasan said, “There are two days and two nights that the creation have not seen the like thereof: the first night one spends with the inhabitants of the grave; the night, which is followed by the morning during which the Hour will arrive; the day that the bearer of news will come to you - either with tidings of the Paradise or Hellfire; and the day that you are given your book, either in your right (hand) or in your left (hand).” Abu Bakr Ibn Abu Ad-Dunyaa reported this saying. It is related from many other eminent ones that they would give great significance to the night which is followed by the morning during which the Hour arrives.

258 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

Humaid said, “During a day from Rajab, as Al-Hasan was in the Masjid, he had a Qaleelah in his hand. He sucked out its water and then spit it out. Then he breathed heavily and cried, so much so that he made his cushion shake. He said, ‘Would that hearts had life to them! Would that hearts were in a healthy state! O, woe to you from a night, whose following morning is the Day of Resurrection! The creation has never heard of a day when private areas will be more exposed and when eyes will shed more tears than the Day of Resurrection.” Ibn Abu Ad-Dunyaa related this narration.

On The Day Of Resurrection, The First For Whom The Earth Will Split Open Is The Messenger Of Allah 2

Abu Hurairah 2s reported that the Messenger of Allah 2 said, “Iam the chief of the children of Adam on the Day of Resurrection and the first for whom the earth will split open. I am the first to intercede and the first whose intercession will be accepted.” (Muslim)

Abu Sa’eed reported that the Messenger of Allah 2 said, “I am the chief of the children of Adam on the Day of Resurrection, and no Fakhr (no pride or haughtiness because of that). And I am the first for whom the earth will split open, and no Fakhr.” Hasheem related this narration.

Abu Hurairah # reported that the Messenger of Allah 2% said, “The horn will be blown, and those in the heavens and the earth will swoon away, except for those whom Allah 8 exempts. Then it will be blown another time, and I will be the first to be resurrected, (and I will see) Moosa holding on to (a part of) the Throne. And I will not know, was he recompensed for when he swooned away on the Day of Toor (when he #% swooned away after Allah 3 revealed Himself to the mountain) or was he resurrected before me.” (Related by Abu Bakr ibn Abu Ad-Dunyaa)

There is a narration in As-sahih that is similar to this in wording. The hadith is in sahih Muslim: “I am the first for whom the earth will split open, and I will find Moosa, strongly holding on to the Qaaimah (foot or post) of the Throne. I do not know — did he get up before me? Or was he rewarded for when he swooned away at AtToor.” Here, one hadith is intermingled with another, for there seems to be no explanation for the reiteration, especially considering

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 259

his saying, “Or was he rewarded for when he swooned away at AtToor (i.e. this saying apparently does not correspond in the given context with “Did he get up before me?”).”

Sa’eed Ibn Al-Musayyib said, “There was a dispute between Abu Bakr # and a Jew. The Jew said, ‘By the One Who has chosen Moosa over all of mankind.’ Abu Bakr .4 slapped him. The Jew went to the Messenger of Allah #, who said, ‘O Jew! | am the first for whom the earth will split open, and I will find Moosa clinging to the Throne. And I will not know — was he before me? Or was he rewarded for his swooning away?” Abu Bakr Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa related this narration, and through the particular chain of his narration, it is Mursal. But it is also related through different chains and with different wordings in sahih Al-Bukhaaree and sahih Muslin. In some of those narrations, it is not Abu Bakr .& who is speaking to the Jew, but rather it is a man from the Ansaar. And Allah # knows best.

And from the best phrasings of the hadith is this one: “On the Day of Resurrection, people will swoon away, and I will be the first to (get up after) swooning away, and I will find Moosa strongly holding on to a Qaaimah (foot or pole) from the Qaaimahs of the Throne. And I will not know — did he swoon away and then get up before me? Or was he rewarded for when he swooned away at AtToor (i.e., rewarded by not having to swoon away a second time)?”

As we will further clarify later on, this swooning away or passing out will occur on the ‘Arasaat (vast plane) of the Resurrection, so it is different from the swooning away that is mentioned in the Quran. People will swoon away on the Day of Resurrection when Allah & reveals Himself (i.e. His Shin) to them, which will occur when He 8% comes to render judgments. People will pass out just as Moosa S2é) passed out on the Day of At-Toor. And Allah a knows best.

Al-Hasan 4 reported that the Messenger of Allah #8 said, “It is as if Isee myself, shaking the dirt off my head. I will turn around and not see anyone except for Moosa, who will be clinging to the Throne. I will not know whether he is from those whom Allah exempted from being afflicted by the blow (of the horn) or whether he was resurrected before me.” (Related by Abu Bakr ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) This narration is Mursal also, but it is even weaker than the other one.

‘Abdullah ibn Salaam 4 reported that the Messenger of Allah 2%

260 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

said, “I am the chief of the children of Adam on the Day of Resurrection, and no Fakir (no pride or haughtiness), and I am the first for whom the earth will split open. I am the first to intercede and the first whose intercession will be accepted. In my hand will be the banner of Al-Hamd, (ander which will be) even Adam and those below him.” (Related by Al-Haafiz Abu Bakr Al-Baihaqee) They (i.e. the other compilers of the Sunnah) did not relate this, though its chain is okay.

Ibn ‘Umar # reported that the Messenger of Allah $ said, “I am the first for whom the earth will split open; then Abu Bakr; then ‘Umar. Then I will go the people of Al-Baqee’ (famous graveyard in AlMadeenah), and they will be gathered with me. Then | will wait for the people of Makkah, and they will be gathered with me. The gathering with me, then, will be between Al-Haramain (i.e. between Makkah and Al-Madeenah).” (Related by Abu Bakr ibn Abu AdDunyaa)

Ibn ‘Umar 4 said, “The Messenger of Allah 2% entered the Masjid; Abu Bakr was on his right and ‘Umar was on his left, and he was leaning on both of them. He #% then said, ‘This is how we will be taised on the Day of Resurrection.” (Related by Abu Bakr Ibn Abu Ad-Dunyaa)
content_id:
Dalam bab di mana kami menceritakan gambaran Surga, kami akan menyebutkan hadith ini beserta berbagai sanadnya dan riwayat-riwayat lain yang menguatkannya. Dan kami memohon pertolongan dari Allah #é.

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Bencana Besar 257

Jasad Para Nabi Tidak Hancur Di Dalam Bumi

Umaamah ibn Al-Mundhir 4 meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah #& bersabda, "Penghulu segala hari adalah hari Jumat. Ia adalah hari yang paling mulia di sisi Allah, lebih mulia di sisi Allah daripada hari Al-Fitr dan hari Al-Adhaa. Padanya terdapat lima keistimewaan: pada hari itu Allah menciptakan Adam; pada hari itu Allah mewafatkan Adam; padanya terdapat suatu waktu, di mana tidaklah seorang hamba meminta sesuatu kepada Allah, melainkan Allah mengabulkannya — selama ia tidak meminta sesuatu yang haram; dan pada hari itu Kiamat akan terjadi. Setiap malaikat Muqarrab (yang dipilih dan dekat), setiap langit, setiap bumi, setiap gunung, dan setiap lautan Yushfiqu (merasa takut) akan hari Jumat (yakni, takut bahwa pada hari Jumat yang ada di hadapan mereka, Kiamat akan tiba)." (Ahmad) Ibn Maajah pun meriwayatkannya. Berikut ini adalah riwayat yang disandarkan Ibn 'Umar 4% kepada Nabi i: "Kiamat akan terjadi pada azan Fajr, pada hari Jumat." At-Tabaraanee meriwayatkan riwayat ini. Dalam At-Tadhkirah, Abu 'Abdullah Al-Qurtubee menyebutkan bahwa itu terjadi pada hari Jumat, di pertengahan Ramadan. Namun hal ini memerlukan bukti yang kuat.

Al-Hasan berkata, "Ada dua hari dan dua malam yang belum pernah dilihat semisal oleh makhluk: malam pertama yang seseorang lalui bersama penghuni kubur; malam yang setelahnya tiba pagi hari di mana Kiamat akan terjadi; hari di mana pembawa berita akan datang kepadamu — baik dengan kabar Surga maupun Neraka; dan hari di mana engkau diberikan kitabmu, baik di tangan kanan maupun di tangan kirimu." Abu Bakr Ibn Abu Ad-Dunyaa meriwayatkan perkataan ini. Diriwayatkan dari banyak tokoh terkemuka lainnya bahwa mereka sangat mementingkan malam yang setelahnya tiba pagi hari di mana Kiamat akan terjadi.

258 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Bencana Besar

Humaid berkata, "Pada suatu hari di bulan Rajab, ketika Al-Hasan sedang berada di Masjid, ia memegang sebuah Qaleelah di tangannya. Ia menghisap airnya lalu memuntahkannya. Kemudian ia menarik napas panjang dan menangis, hingga membuat bantalnya bergetar. Ia berkata, 'Seandainya hati-hati memiliki kehidupan! Seandainya hati-hati berada dalam keadaan sehat! Wahai, celakalah engkau dari suatu malam, yang paginya adalah Hari Kiamat! Makhluk tidak pernah mendengar tentang suatu hari di mana aurat akan lebih terbuka dan di mana mata akan lebih banyak mencucurkan air mata selain Hari Kiamat." Ibn Abu Ad-Dunyaa meriwayatkan riwayat ini.

Pada Hari Kiamat, Orang Pertama Yang Bumi Akan Terbelah Untuknya Adalah Rasulullah 2

Abu Hurairah 2s meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah 2 bersabda, "Aku adalah pemimpin anak cucu Adam pada Hari Kiamat dan orang pertama yang bumi akan terbelah untuknya. Aku adalah orang pertama yang memberikan syafaat dan orang pertama yang syafaatnya akan diterima." (Muslim)

Abu Sa'eed meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah 2 bersabda, "Aku adalah pemimpin anak cucu Adam pada Hari Kiamat, dan tiada Fakhr (tiada kebanggaan atau kesombongan karena itu). Dan aku adalah orang pertama yang bumi akan terbelah untuknya, dan tiada Fakhr." Hasheem meriwayatkan riwayat ini.

Abu Hurairah # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah 2% bersabda, "Sangkakala akan ditiup, dan orang-orang yang ada di langit dan di bumi akan pingsan, kecuali mereka yang dikecualikan oleh Allah 8. Kemudian ditiup lagi untuk kedua kalinya, dan aku akan menjadi orang pertama yang dibangkitkan, (dan aku akan melihat) Musa memegang (salah satu bagian dari) Arasy. Dan aku tidak tahu, apakah ia mendapat balasan atas pingsannya pada Hari Toor (ketika ia #% pingsan setelah Allah 3 menampakkan Diri-Nya kepada gunung itu) ataukah ia dibangkitkan sebelum aku." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Bakr ibn Abu Ad-Dunyaa)

Terdapat riwayat dalam As-Sahih yang serupa dengan ini dalam redaksinya. Hadith tersebut ada dalam Sahih Muslim: "Aku adalah orang pertama yang bumi akan terbelah untuknya, dan aku akan mendapati Musa, yang sedang berpegang erat pada Qaaimah (kaki atau tiang) Arasy. Aku tidak tahu — apakah ia bangkit sebelum aku? Ataukah ia mendapat balasan atas pingsannya di At-Toor." Di sini, satu hadith bercampur dengan hadith lainnya, karena tampaknya tidak ada penjelasan atas pengulangan tersebut, terutama mempertimbangkan

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Bencana Besar 259

sabda beliau, "Ataukah ia mendapat balasan atas pingsannya di At-Toor (yakni, sabda ini tampaknya tidak sesuai dalam konteks yang diberikan dengan 'Apakah ia bangkit sebelum aku?')."

Sa'eed Ibn Al-Musayyib berkata, "Terjadi perselisihan antara Abu Bakr # dan seorang Yahudi. Orang Yahudi itu berkata, 'Demi Dzat yang telah memilih Musa di atas seluruh umat manusia.' Abu Bakr .4 menamparnya. Orang Yahudi itu pergi menemui Rasulullah #, yang bersabda, 'Wahai orang Yahudi! Aku adalah orang pertama yang bumi akan terbelah untuknya, dan aku akan mendapati Musa sedang berpegang pada Arasy. Dan aku tidak tahu — apakah ia sebelum aku? Ataukah ia mendapat balasan atas pingsannya?' Abu Bakr Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa meriwayatkan riwayat ini, dan melalui sanad khusus riwayatnya, ia berstatus Mursal. Namun ia juga diriwayatkan melalui berbagai sanad dan dengan redaksi yang berbeda dalam Sahih Al-Bukhaaree dan Sahih Muslim. Dalam sebagian riwayat tersebut, yang berbicara kepada orang Yahudi bukan Abu Bakr .&, melainkan seorang lelaki dari kaum Ansaar. Dan Allah # lebih mengetahui.

Dan termasuk redaksi hadith yang terbaik adalah yang berikut ini: "Pada Hari Kiamat, orang-orang akan pingsan, dan aku akan menjadi orang pertama yang (bangkit setelah) pingsan, dan aku akan mendapati Musa sedang berpegang erat pada sebuah Qaaimah (kaki atau tiang) dari Qaaimah-Qaaimah Arasy. Dan aku tidak tahu — apakah ia pingsan lalu bangkit sebelum aku? Ataukah ia mendapat balasan atas pingsannya di At-Toor (yakni, diberi balasan dengan tidak harus pingsan untuk kedua kalinya)?"

Sebagaimana akan kami perjelas lebih lanjut, pingsan ini akan terjadi di 'Arasaat (hamparan luas) Kebangkitan, sehingga ia berbeda dari pingsan yang disebutkan dalam Quran. Orang-orang akan pingsan pada Hari Kiamat ketika Allah & menampakkan Diri-Nya (yakni Betis-Nya) kepada mereka, yang akan terjadi ketika Ia 8% datang untuk memberikan penghakiman. Orang-orang akan pingsan sebagaimana Musa S2é) pingsan pada Hari At-Toor. Dan Allah a lebih mengetahui.

Al-Hasan 4 meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah #8 bersabda, "Seolah-olah aku melihat diriku sedang mengibaskan debu dari kepalaku. Aku akan menoleh dan tidak melihat siapapun kecuali Musa, yang sedang berpegang pada Arasy. Aku tidak tahu apakah ia termasuk orang-orang yang dikecualikan Allah dari terkena tiupan (sangkakala) ataukah ia dibangkitkan sebelum aku." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Bakr ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) Riwayat ini juga berstatus Mursal, bahkan lebih lemah dari riwayat sebelumnya.

'Abdullah ibn Salaam 4 meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah 2%

260 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Bencana Besar

bersabda, "Aku adalah pemimpin anak cucu Adam pada Hari Kiamat, dan tiada Fakhr (tiada kebanggaan atau kesombongan), dan aku adalah orang pertama yang bumi akan terbelah untuknya. Aku adalah orang pertama yang memberikan syafaat dan orang pertama yang syafaatnya akan diterima. Di tanganku akan ada panji Al-Hamd, (yang di bawahnya berada) bahkan Adam dan mereka yang di bawahnya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Haafiz Abu Bakr Al-Baihaqee) Mereka (yakni para perawi Sunnah lainnya) tidak meriwayatkannya, meskipun sanadnya cukup baik.

Ibn 'Umar # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah $ bersabda, "Aku adalah orang pertama yang bumi akan terbelah untuknya; kemudian Abu Bakr; kemudian 'Umar. Kemudian aku akan pergi kepada penduduk Al-Baqee' (pemakaman terkenal di Al-Madeenah), dan mereka akan dikumpulkan bersamaku. Kemudian aku akan menunggu penduduk Makkah, dan mereka akan dikumpulkan bersamaku. Pengumpulan bersamaku, maka, akan berlangsung antara Al-Haramain (yakni antara Makkah dan Al-Madeenah)." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Bakr ibn Abu Ad-Dunyaa)

Ibn 'Umar 4 berkata, "Rasulullah 2% memasuki Masjid; Abu Bakr berada di sebelah kanannya dan 'Umar berada di sebelah kirinya, dan beliau bersandar pada keduanya. Beliau #% kemudian bersabda, 'Demikianlah kita akan dibangkitkan pada Hari Kiamat.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Bakr Ibn Abu Ad-Dunyaa)
content_ar: In the الباب wherein we relate the description of الجنة, we will mention this الحديث with its different chains and with other narrations that attest to it. And we seek help from الله #é. الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 257 The Bodies Of The الأنبياء Do Not Decompose In The Earth Umaamah ibn Al-Mundhir 4 reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله #& said, “The chief of the days is Friday. It is the most superior with الله, more superior with الله than the day of Al-Fitr and the day of Al-Adhaa. It has in it five qualities: خلال it, الله created آدم; خلال it الله made آدم die; خلال it is an hour, in which no slave asks anything from الله, except that الله gives it to him - as long as he does not ask for something الحرام; and on it, the Hour will arrive. Every Mugarrib (chosen and close) angel, every heaven, every earth, every mountain, and every sea Yushfiqu (fears) Friday (Le, fears that on the Friday قبل them, the Hour will arrive).” (أحمد) Ibn Maajah related it as well, The following is a narration that Ibn ‘عمر 4% ascribed to النبي ﷺ i: “The Hour will arrive at the Fajr Aadhaan, on Friday.” At-Tabaraanee related this narration. In AtTadhkirah, Abu ‘Abdullah Al-Qurtubee related that it is on a Friday, halfway through Ramadan. But this requires some proof. Al-حسن said, “There are two days and two nights that the creation have not seen the like thereof: the أولاً night one spends with the inhabitants of the القبر; the night, which is followed by the morning خلال which the Hour will arrive; the day that the bearer of news will come to you - either with tidings of the الجنة or النار; and the day that you are given your الكتاب, either in your الحق (hand) or in your left (hand).” أبو بكر Ibn Abu Ad-Dunyaa reported this saying. It is related from many other eminent ones that they would give great significance to the night which is followed by the morning خلال which the Hour arrives. 258 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations Humaid said, “خلال a day from Rajab, as Al-حسن was in the المسجد, he had a Qaleelah in his hand. He sucked out its water and then spit it out. Then he breathed heavily and cried, لذا much لذا that he made his cushion shake. قال, ‘Would that القلوب had الحياة to them! Would that القلوب were in a healthy state! O, woe to you from a night, whose following morning is the Day of البعث! The creation has never heard of a day عندما private areas will be more exposed and عندما eyes will shed more tears than the Day of البعث.” Ibn Abu Ad-Dunyaa related this narration. On The Day Of البعث, The أولاً For Whom The Earth Will Split Open Is الرسول ﷺ Of الله 2 أبو هريرة 2s reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2 said, “Iam the chief of the الأولاد of آدم on the Day of البعث and the أولاً for whom the earth will split open. I am the أولاً to intercede and the أولاً whose intercession will be accepted.” (مسلم) Abu Sa’eed reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2 said, “I am the chief of the الأولاد of آدم on the Day of البعث, and no Fakhr (no الكبر or haughtiness لأن of that). And I am the أولاً for whom the earth will split open, and no Fakhr.” Hasheem related this narration. أبو هريرة # reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% said, “The horn will be blown, and those in the heavens and the earth will swoon away, except for those whom الله 8 exempts. Then it will be blown another time, and I will be the أولاً to be resurrected, (and I will see) Moosa holding on to (a part of) the Throne. And I will not know, was he recompensed for عندما he swooned away on the Day of Toor (عندما he #% swooned away بعد الله 3 revealed Himself to the mountain) or was he resurrected قبل me.” (Related by أبو بكر ibn Abu Ad-Dunyaa) There is a narration in As-صحيح that is similar to this in wording. The الحديث is in صحيح مسلم: “I am the أولاً for whom the earth will split open, and I will find Moosa, strongly holding on to the Qaaimah (foot or post) of the Throne. I do not know — did he get up قبل me? Or was he rewarded for عندما he swooned away at AtToor.” Here, one الحديث is intermingled with another, for there seems to be no explanation for the reiteration, ولا سيما considering الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 259 his saying, “Or was he rewarded for عندما he swooned away at AtToor (i.e. this saying apparently does not correspond in the given context with “Did he get up قبل me?”).” Sa’eed Ibn Al-Musayyib said, “There was a dispute between أبو بكر # and a Jew. The Jew said, ‘By the One Who has chosen Moosa over all of mankind.’ أبو بكر .4 slapped him. The Jew went to الرسول ﷺ of الله #, who said, ‘O Jew! | am the أولاً for whom the earth will split open, and I will find Moosa clinging to the Throne. And I will not know — was he قبل me? Or was he rewarded for his swooning away?” أبو بكر Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa related this narration, and through the particular chain of his narration, it is Mursal. But it is أيضاً related through different chains and with different wordings in صحيح البخاري and صحيح Muslin. In some of those narrations, it is not أبو بكر .& who is speaking to the Jew, but rather it is a man from the Ansaar. And الله # knows best. And from the best phrasings of the الحديث is this one: “On the Day of البعث, people will swoon away, and I will be the أولاً to (get up بعد) swooning away, and I will find Moosa strongly holding on to a Qaaimah (foot or pole) from the Qaaimahs of the Throne. And I will not know — did he swoon away and then get up قبل me? Or was he rewarded for عندما he swooned away at AtToor (i.e., rewarded by not having to swoon away a ثانياً time)?” As we will further clarify later on, this swooning away or passing out will occur on the ‘Arasaat (vast plane) of the البعث, لذا it is different from the swooning away that is mentioned in the القرآن الكريم. People will swoon away on the Day of البعث عندما الله & reveals Himself (i.e. His Shin) to them, which will occur عندما He 8% comes to render judgments. People will pass out just as Moosa S2é) passed out on the Day of At-Toor. And الله a knows best. Al-حسن 4 reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله #8 said, “It is as إذا Isee myself, shaking the dirt off my head. I will turn around and not see anyone except for Moosa, who will be clinging to the Throne. I will not know whether he is from those whom الله exempted from being afflicted by the blow (of the horn) or whether he was resurrected قبل me.” (Related by أبو بكر ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) This narration is Mursal أيضاً, but it is even weaker than the other one. ‘Abdullah ibn Salaam 4 reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% 260 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations said, “I am the chief of the الأولاد of آدم on the Day of البعث, and no Fakir (no الكبر or haughtiness), and I am the أولاً for whom the earth will split open. I am the أولاً to intercede and the أولاً whose intercession will be accepted. In my hand will be the banner of Al-Hamd, (ander which will be) even آدم and those below him.” (Related by Al-Haafiz أبو بكر Al-Baihaqee) They (i.e. the other compilers of the السنة) did not relate this, though its chain is okay. Ibn ‘عمر # reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله $ said, “I am the أولاً for whom the earth will split open; then أبو بكر; then ‘عمر. Then I will go the people of Al-Baqee’ (famous graveyard in AlMadeenah), and they will be gathered with me. Then | will wait for the people of مكة المكرمة, and they will be gathered with me. The gathering with me, then, will be between Al-Haramain (i.e. between مكة المكرمة and Al-Madeenah).” (Related by أبو بكر ibn Abu AdDunyaa) Ibn ‘عمر 4 said, “الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% entered the المسجد; أبو بكر was on his الحق and ‘عمر was on his left, and he was leaning on both of them. He #% then said, ‘This is how we will be taised on the Day of البعث.” (Related by أبو بكر Ibn Abu Ad-Dunyaa)
